pages.title: Quick Start
capabilities_to_preserve: Sign in or complete the first-run setup flow.
Open the main chat workspace and confirm the app is responsive.
Use the sidebar to move between chat, history, models, jobs, files, analytics, and settings.
Check health, version, and runtime indicators before starting longer work.
notes_to_preserve: If the UI loads but chat does not respond, check runtime status, provider credentials, and logs before changing settings.
Avoid pasting secrets into chat or screenshots; configure credentials through the intended settings or environment path.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Interface Overview
capabilities_to_preserve: Understand the main navigation areas and what each one is for.
Recognize which pages are for daily use and which are for administration.
Know where to look when a feature depends on runtime, credentials, or external services.
notes_to_preserve: Some pages can run commands, write files, schedule work, or connect external channels; review the action before confirming.
If you are documenting or debugging behavior, use screenshots plus logs rather than relying on memory.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Chat and Sessions
capabilities_to_preserve: Start a new conversation or continue an existing session.
Switch models before sending a message.
Use Markdown and tool output rendering to review structured responses.
Open related files or a terminal drawer when a task needs local context.
Search across current work and jump to relevant sessions.
notes_to_preserve: Model changes usually affect the next turn, not past answers.
Terminal and file drawers can expose or modify local data; use them deliberately.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: History and Search
capabilities_to_preserve: Browse recent sessions and open a specific conversation.
Search by title, content, or relevant terms.
Use deep links to return to a session view.
Distinguish saved history from the currently open chat session.
notes_to_preserve: Search is useful for recall, but stale sessions may not reflect current files or settings.
Use the session content as context; verify current project state before acting on old instructions.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Models and Providers
capabilities_to_preserve: Review configured providers and available models.
Add or adjust custom provider entries when supported.
Choose defaults and aliases used by the chat experience.
Check whether provider authentication appears ready.
notes_to_preserve: Provider credentials are sensitive. Do not expose tokens in screenshots, chat, or issue reports.
If a model is missing, check provider configuration, credentials, visibility settings, and server logs.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Profiles
capabilities_to_preserve: Create, clone, import, export, and switch profiles.
Keep provider and channel settings isolated by profile.
Review profile-specific status before starting work.
notes_to_preserve: Switching profiles can change credentials, models, gateways, skills, and memory behavior.
Avoid editing production-like profiles when experimenting; clone first if unsure.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Group Chat
capabilities_to_preserve: Create and open group chat rooms.
Add or review room members.
Mention participants to direct a question or task.
Watch message flow and participant status.
notes_to_preserve: Multi-agent rooms are only as useful as their configured members and runtime availability.
Do not put secrets in group chat unless every configured participant and channel is appropriate for that data.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Kanban
capabilities_to_preserve: View tasks by status column.
Create new tasks with enough context to execute.
Open task details and update status.
Use the board as an operational overview for ongoing work.
notes_to_preserve: Kanban is a coordination tool; it does not replace verification. Mark work complete only after the result is checked.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Jobs and Cron
capabilities_to_preserve: Create scheduled jobs with prompts, skills, scripts, or delivery targets.
Pause, resume, run, edit, or remove jobs.
Review job history and output.
Use jobs for monitoring, briefings, reminders, or repeated workflows.
notes_to_preserve: Jobs can execute scripts, call tools, or send messages. Review prompts and delivery targets carefully before enabling automation.
Prefer smoke-testing a job before letting it run unattended.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Files and Workspaces
capabilities_to_preserve: Browse workspace folders and files.
Preview common text and document formats.
Upload or download files.
Edit, rename, copy, move, or delete supported files.
Use files alongside Chat when a task needs project context.
notes_to_preserve: File actions mutate workspace data. Verify paths before editing or deleting.
Do not expose private files in screenshots or shared manual examples.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Skills, Memory, and Plugins
capabilities_to_preserve: Browse available skills and read skill details.
Review memory or notes exposed through the UI.
Inspect plugin inventory and availability.
Use skills as reusable procedures for recurring tasks.
notes_to_preserve: Memory and skills can influence future assistant behavior. Treat personal, credential, or project-sensitive entries carefully.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Usage and Analytics
capabilities_to_preserve: Review usage and cost trends.
Inspect model and cache activity.
Monitor performance over time.
See which skills are used and when.
notes_to_preserve: Analytics are best for trends and investigations; confirm billing or provider-side totals with the provider when precision matters.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Channels and Gateways
capabilities_to_preserve: Review connected channel status.
Configure supported gateway settings.
Understand where replies or job output may be delivered.
Diagnose channel connection issues.
notes_to_preserve: Channel settings can send messages outside the web UI. Confirm recipients and tokens before enabling delivery.
Never publish screenshots that expose bot tokens, webhook URLs, or private channel identifiers.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Settings and Security
capabilities_to_preserve: Adjust account and display preferences.
Review privacy, memory, and session-related settings.
Configure voice or accessibility-related options when available.
Understand which settings may affect credentials or sensitive data.
notes_to_preserve: Settings can affect security, retention, and external integrations. Avoid changing multiple unrelated settings at once when troubleshooting.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Web Terminal
capabilities_to_preserve: Open a browser terminal session.
Run commands in the configured environment.
Use multiple sessions or resized terminal panes when supported.
Pair terminal output with Chat or file inspection.
notes_to_preserve: Terminal commands can read or modify local files and services. Do not paste secrets, destructive commands, or credentials into shared contexts.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Coding Agents, Global Agent, and MCP
capabilities_to_preserve: Start or review coding-agent runs.
Open Global Agent sessions.
Add, inspect, or manage MCP servers.
Understand which tools are available before asking an agent to use them.
notes_to_preserve: Advanced agent tools may read files, run commands, call external services, or modify workspaces. Use narrow instructions and review results.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Devices, Version, and Logs
capabilities_to_preserve: Inspect linked or discoverable devices.
Review version preview or update information.
Open logs while diagnosing failures.
Use runtime evidence when reporting issues.
notes_to_preserve: Logs may include paths, profile names, channel identifiers, or error details. Review before sharing.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Docker and Deployment
capabilities_to_preserve: Install dependencies and start the development or production server.
Use Docker Compose when containerized deployment is preferred.
Understand persistent storage and environment variables at a high level.
Check health endpoints and logs after startup.
notes_to_preserve: Do not put credentials in screenshots or shared compose files. Use environment management appropriate for your deployment.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Troubleshooting
capabilities_to_preserve: Narrow a problem to UI, server, runtime, provider, profile, workspace, or channel configuration.
Use logs, health checks, and status pages to collect evidence.
Fix common startup, authentication, provider, WebSocket, terminal, and Docker issues.
notes_to_preserve: Do not paste full logs publicly without checking for tokens, local paths, profile names, or private message content.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Technical Appendix: API and Route Map
capabilities_to_preserve: Keep the Technical Appendix: API and Route Map page accurate for user and agent navigation.
notes_to_preserve: Keep this page concise and aligned with current release coverage.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users and agents; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

pages.title: Screenshot Gallery
capabilities_to_preserve: Browse every manual screenshot in one place.
Map screenshot filenames to product areas.
Use screenshots as visual references during review or support.
notes_to_preserve: The gallery uses demo/manual screenshots. Treat visible data as example content.
pages.rewrite_goal: English product manual page for end users; feature-first, natural, concise, screenshot-aware.

capabilities_to_preserve: Keep the Technical Appendix: Release Sync Notes page accurate for user and agent navigation.
notes_to_preserve: Keep this page concise and aligned with current release coverage.
